senses.gloss: Name of the 25th letter of the Arabic alphabet (ن / ن), representing the sound /n/.
to add nunation to (a word), to nunate (a word)
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include

examples.text: وَذَا النُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغٰضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَى فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ أَن لَّا إِلٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ سُبْحٰنَكَ إِنِّي كُنْتُ مِنَ الظّٰلِمِينَ
examples.english: And the man of the nūn, when he went off in fury and opined that We would not decree upon him. And he cried in the glooms “there is no god short of You, exalted are You. Indeed I have been of the wrongdoers.”

senses.gloss: The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; fish; great fish
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; a male given name from Hebrew
A tradition in Kuwait in celebration of an event in which a person throws candy and money from atop for children and other attendees to collect

etymology.text:
Deverbative from the letter نُون (nūn).

Borrowed from Classical Syriac ܢܽܘܢܳܐ (nūnā, “fish”) as in the Hebrew story of Joshua, son of Nun, ultimately likely from Akkadian 𒄩 (nūnum), possibly from Sumerian.
